Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "duplicate" or "housing" or "wrong city" (or similar) to find the initial incident reports. Expected findings: Lena's email to Samira about duplicate query results from the relocation-matching endpoint, and Chloe's email to Samira (CC Lena) reporting that Simone Richter from BrightLoop Analytics received a housing confirmation for a Chicago apartment (1847 N Milwaukee Ave, Unit 4B) instead of her correct Boston destination.
OE 2: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with search_query related to "duplicate" or "query optimization" or "stale" or "migration" (or similar) to find the engineering team discussion about the problem. Expected findings: Thread in #engineering (C003) where Lena reports duplicate results from stale company records with shortened names, Samira asks to scope the full impact, Dmitri confirms duplicates exist with shorter names scoring higher on fuzzy matching, and Lena files ENG-483.
OE 3: Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues with query related to "ghost" or "migration" or "remediation" or "duplicate" (or similar), filtering by team "team_engineering" (or similar), to find the investigation ticket. Expected findings: ENG-483 (id: issue_ghost_records_remediation) titled "Investigate and remediate Wanderlust migration ghost records", state in_progress, assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom. Description mentions stale records from system consolidation causing wrong city housing confirmation. Two comments from Dmitri Volkov and Lena: Dmitri's analysis of the fuzzy matching logic favoring shorter company names, and Lena's note about starting the full inventory and checking ops records and billing side.
OE 4: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to discover the ghost company record alongside the canonical one. Expected findings: Two results - canonical "BrightLoop Analytics" (company_brightloop, Boston, domain brightloopanalytics.com) and ghost "BrightLoop" (ghost_company_brightloop, Chicago, domain brightloop.com, type PROSPECT, created 2024-08-15, description mentions "Migrated from Wanderlust CRM during Q3 2024 system consolidation").
OE 5: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "Canopy" (or similar) to check for additional ghost records. Expected findings: Two results - canonical "Canopy Health" (company_canopy, Chicago, domain canopyhealth.com) and ghost "Canopy" (ghost_company_canopy, Chicago, domain canopyhealth.org, type PROSPECT, created 2024-08-15, description mentions Wanderlust migration). Note: Unlike BrightLoop where the ghost has a different city (Chicago vs Boston), both Canopy records are in Chicago. The ghost is distinguished by its shortened name ("Canopy" vs "Canopy Health"), different domain (.org vs .com), and different address (875 N Michigan Ave vs 200 W Adams St).
OE 6: Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies with name "GreenStack" (or similar) to check for additional ghost records. Expected findings: Two results - canonical "GreenStack Energy" (company_greenstack, Seattle, domain greenstackenergy.com) and ghost "GreenStack" (ghost_company_greenstack, Seattle, domain greenstack.io, type PROSPECT, created 2024-08-15, description mentions Wanderlust migration).
OE 7: Search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts with full_name "Simone Richter" (or similar) to find ghost contact records linked to ghost companies. Expected findings: Two results - canonical contact (simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com, company BrightLoop Analytics, Data Platform Lead) and ghost contact (srichter@brightloop.com, company BrightLoop, Chicago, Senior Data Analyst, created 2024-08-15).
OE 8: Search CRM contacts for other ghost contacts linked to ghost companies by searching for "Marcus Webb", "Patricia Langford", "Derek Hollis", or "Priya Venkatesh" (or similar). Expected findings: Ghost contacts for Marcus Webb (mwebb@brightloop.com under BrightLoop), Patricia Langford (plangford@canopyhealth.org under Canopy), Derek Hollis (dhollis@greenstack.io under GreenStack), and Priya Venkatesh (pvenkatesh@greenstack.io under GreenStack).
OE 9: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations", field_name "Company", and value "BrightLoop" or similar) or airtable_list_records (with base_id, table_name "Relocations") to find ghost relocation templates. The agent may first need to call airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to discover the base_id and table names. Expected findings: Three ghost relocation templates - recGhostBrightloopChicago (Simone Richter, Company "BrightLoop", Origin "San Francisco, CA" to Destination "Chicago, IL", Status Completed, housing address 1847 N Milwaukee Ave, Unit 4B), recGhostGreenStackSeattle (Priya Venkatesh, Company "GreenStack", Origin Houston TX to Destination Seattle WA, Status Completed), and recGhostCanopyChicago (Dr. Yusuf Abdi, Company "Canopy", Origin Minneapolis MN to Destination Chicago IL, Status Completed). All have Special Requirements noting "Migrated from Wanderlust CRM."
OE 10: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria array filtering on DisplayName containing "UrbanNest" (or similar) to discover the vendor duplication. Expected findings: Two vendor entries - canonical "UrbanNest Corporate Housing" (Id: vendor_urbannest) and legacy "UrbanNest LLC" (Id: vendor_urbannest_legacy, CompanyName "UrbanNest LLC", Chicago billing address, Balance $6,850, MetaData shows CreateTime 2024-08-15).
OE 11: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to the legacy vendor or "UrbanNest" (or similar) to find misrouted bills. Expected findings: Three bills on vendor_urbannest_legacy totaling $6,850 - UN-LEGACY-0421 ($2,850 for Simone Richter Boston temp + Jordan Whitfield Denver temp), UN-LEGACY-0418 ($1,900 for Marcus Webb Boston temp), and UN-LEGACY-0422 ($2,100 for Mei-Lin Zhao Portland temp + Joaquin Reyes Seattle temp).
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "BrightLoop" or "Tessa" or "data accuracy" or "housing mix-up" (or similar) to find client complaints and internal reports. Expected findings: (a) Email from Tessa Moreno (tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com) to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, CC david.chen@moveops.com, expressing concern about Simone Richter's wrong housing confirmation for 1847 N Milwaukee Ave Unit 4B in Chicago, Marcus Webb seeing duplicate portal entries (one under "BrightLoop Analytics" correct and one under "BrightLoop" showing Chicago), and 4 additional relocations planned for Q3 contingent on data accuracy before expanding the contract. (b) Email from Mina Hashimoto to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com) about Tessa's call, Marcus Webb's Airtable record looking correct but portal potentially matching stale data, and client retention risk given BrightLoop is expanding their contract.
OE 13: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "GreenStack" or "Derek" or "duplicate records" (or similar) to find additional client complaints. Expected findings: Email from Derek Hollis (derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com) to Emeka Diallo reporting that Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes both see duplicate entries in the relocation portal (one under "GreenStack Energy" correct, one under "GreenStack" outdated).
OE 14: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements with company_ids for BrightLoop and GreenStack (or similar) to find logged client impact notes. Expected findings: Engagement note for BrightLoop about Simone Richter housing error and Tessa Moreno's concerns, and engagement note for GreenStack about Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes duplicate portal entries.
OE 15: Search emails using search_emails with query related to "vendor" or "UrbanNest" or "QuickBooks" or "billing" or "duplicate" (or similar) to find Marcus's report about the financial side. Expected findings: Email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com (CC lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com) describing two separate UrbanNest entries in QuickBooks ("UrbanNest Corporate Housing" as the current correct record and "UrbanNest LLC" as a Wanderlust-era record with $6,850 balance), recent housing bills totaling $6,850 landing on the legacy entry, vendor reconciliation being wrong, and Hana and Marcus unable to close April books until this is sorted.
OE 16: Search calendar events using calendar_search_events with query related to "remediation" or "migration" or "ghost" or "review" (or similar), or using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start_datetime and end_datetime covering April 27, 2026, to find the Monday review meeting. Expected findings: Calendar event titled "Wanderlust Migration Ghost Records - Remediation Review" on Monday April 27, 2026 at 9:00 AM PT (start_datetime 1777305600), attendees include lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, and chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 17: Add a comment to the existing Linear issue ENG-483 (via linear_create_comment with issueId "issue_ghost_records_remediation") documenting the full inventory of ghost records found: 3 ghost CRM companies (BrightLoop, Canopy, GreenStack), 5 ghost CRM contacts, 3 ghost Airtable relocation templates, 1 legacy QuickBooks vendor (UrbanNest LLC) with $6,850 in misrouted bills, the root cause (Wanderlust migration decommission script skipped records with active QB references, fuzzy matching algorithm favors shorter legacy names), and the client impact (BrightLoop and GreenStack HR contacts both reported issues).
OE 18: Update the ghost Airtable relocation records using airtable_update_records with base_id (discovered via airtable_list_bases), table_id "tblRelocations01", and records array containing the three ghost record IDs. Records to update: recGhostBrightloopChicago, recGhostGreenStackSeattle, recGhostCanopyChicago. The update should modify the Status field or Special Requirements field to indicate these are stale legacy records that should not be used by the ops team (e.g., changing Status to "Cancelled" or adding a warning note like "STALE - Wanderlust migration orphan, do not use" or similar).
OE 19: Create CRM engagement notes (via crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE") on the affected client accounts to log the impact of the ghost records. At minimum, log a note on the BrightLoop Analytics account (company_ids: company_brightloop) documenting the Simone Richter housing error, Marcus Webb duplicate portal entries, and Tessa Moreno's concerns about the Q3 expansion. Also log a note on the GreenStack Energy account (company_ids: company_greenstack) documenting the Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes duplicate portal entries and Derek Hollis's inquiry.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com summarizing the full scope of the ghost records investigation: the complete inventory (3 ghost companies, 5 ghost contacts, 3 ghost Airtable records, 1 legacy QB vendor with $6,850 in misrouted bills), the root cause (Wanderlust migration cleanup script skipped records with active QB references), the client impact (BrightLoop and GreenStack HR contacts flagged issues), and the remediation status (ticket updated, stale records flagged, client impact logged).
OE 21: Post a message to the #engineering Slack channel (via conversations_add_message with channel_id "C003") summarizing the investigation findings and remediation status for the engineering team. The message should cover the scope of ghost records found, the root cause, the client impact, and next steps for the Monday review meeting.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

At minimum, log a note on the BrightLoop Analytics account (company_ids: company_brightloop) documenting the Simone Richter housing error, Marcus Webb duplicate portal entries, and Tessa Moreno's concerns about the Q3 expansion. Also log a note on the GreenStack Energy account (company_ids: company_greenstack) documenting the Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquin Reyes duplicate portal entries and Derek Hollis's inquiry.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com summarizing the full scope of the ghost records investigation: the complete inventory (3 ghost companies, 5 ghost contacts, 3 ghost Airtable records, 1 legacy QB vendor with $6,850 in misrouted bills), the root cause (Wanderlust migration cleanup script skipped records with active QB references), the client impact (BrightLoop and GreenStack HR contacts flagged issues), and the remediation status (ticket updated, stale records flagged, client impact logged).
OE 21: Post a message to the #engineering Slack channel (via conversations_add_message with channel_id "C003") summarizing the investigation findings and remediation status for the engineering team. The message should cover the scope of ghost records found, the root cause, the client impact, and next steps for the Monday review meeting.

21 The Slack message to #engineering mentions the root cause (old migration records not cleaned up, fuzzy matching algorithm favoring shorter names, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Slack message explains the root cause: 'ghost records have shortened company names... The relocation-matching portal's fuzzy matching treats the shorter name as a closer match on edit distance... The cleanup script skipped them because they had active billing references.'
✓Run #2The Slack message states: 'The cleanup script skipped them because they had active billing references in QuickBooks. The portal's matching logic treats the shortened company names (e.g., "BrightLoop" vs "BrightLoop Analytics") as valid matches, so employees see ghost records alongside real ones.' Both the migration cleanup failure and the matching algorithm issue are documented.
✓Run #3The Slack message explains: 'The cleanup script skipped them because QuickBooks had active billing references. Our relocation matching algorithm treats the shorter legacy names as valid matches, so these stale records are being returned alongside — or instead of — the correct ones.'
✓Run #4The Slack message states 'During the Q3 2024 Wanderlust CRM migration, three legacy company records were migrated but never cleaned up' and 'The portal's name-matching logic treats them as valid matches' as the root cause.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The #engineering Slack message explains the root cause: 'The cleanup script that ran post-migration was designed to skip records with active financial references... The portal's fuzzy matching favors shorter legacy names (lower edit distance), so "BrightLoop" scores higher than "BrightLoop Analytics"'
22 The agent must use a CRM tool to investigate company records (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_get_company, or crm_show_data).

28 The agent must search QuickBooks for vendor records or bills using criteria that would surface UrbanNest-related records (e.g., filtering on DisplayName containing "UrbanNest" or similar), rather than relying solely on secondhand information from emails.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓—✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria filtering on DisplayName LIKE '%UrbanNest%' and again with '%Urban%', directly searching QuickBooks data rather than relying solely on email descriptions.
✓Run #2The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria [{'field': 'DisplayName', 'operator': 'LIKE', 'value': '%UrbanNest%'}], directly querying for UrbanNest-related vendor records rather than relying solely on secondhand email information.
✓Run #3quickbooks_search_vendors was called multiple times with criteria for '%UrbanNest%', '%Urban%', and 'UrbanNest' specifically targeting the vendor by name, rather than relying solely on Marcus's email.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria [{'field': 'DisplayName', 'operator': 'LIKE', 'value': 'UrbanNest'}] and quickbooks_search_bills with a VendorRef criteria for UrbanNest, directly querying QuickBooks for UrbanNest-related records rather than relying solely on email descriptions.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_search_vendors with criteria [{'field': 'DisplayName', 'operator': 'LIKE', 'value': '%UrbanNest%'}] to directly query QuickBooks for UrbanNest vendor records, rather than relying solely on email descriptions.
